meta.title: Japanese words

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Every word and meaning transcribed faithfully (including the source's own spelling "Bonzai" for the battle-cry word); context sentences and the teaching layer are authored for CAT OS.

meta.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Word DNA milestone); WORD_DNA_BIBLE frameworks applied
meta.reviewer_notes: Complete Japanese list from the source, ten of ten words. bonzai (the patriotic battle cry, as spelled in the source) and bonsai (the miniature-tree art) are kept as two distinct entries exactly as given, a genuine near-homonym pair worth noticing on its own. Owner review pending.

unit.label: Japanese
unit.mentor_note: Ten words, one country, one small proof that English keeps reaching outward for exactly the word it is missing.

members.context_sentence: A powerful tsunami struck the coastline after the undersea earthquake.
The shipping tycoon owned more vessels than the national navy.

members.meaning: the art of growing miniature trees
members.context_sentence: He spent thirty years shaping a single bonsai in a shallow clay pot.

members.meaning: a patriotic battle cry or shout
members.context_sentence: The soldiers raised their cry of bonzai as they charged forward.

members.meaning: the Japanese art of paper folding
members.context_sentence: She folded a small paper crane using a traditional origami pattern.

members.meaning: a member of the former Japanese warrior class
members.context_sentence: The museum displayed a suit of armor once worn by a samurai.

members.meaning: a traditional Japanese form of unarmed combat
members.context_sentence: She has practiced karate every Tuesday evening since she was seven.
Her grandmother still wears a silk kimono at New Year gatherings.

members.meaning: a hereditary military commander in feudal Japan
members.context_sentence: For centuries, the shogun held more real power in Japan than the emperor did.

members.meaning: a portable charcoal grill or heater
members.context_sentence: They cooked the vegetables over a small hibachi on the porch.

discovery.notice_prompt: English borrowed every one of these words from Japanese. Read each one in its sentence, then guess before the reveal.

predict_options.text: A tropical storm with heavy rain

discovery.understand_note: Tsu means harbor and nami means wave in Japanese. English borrowed the word whole because no existing English word captured a wave born from the sea floor itself, only from the wind above it.

applies.prompt: "For centuries, the shogun held more real power in Japan than the emperor did." What is a shogun?

options.text: A hereditary military commander who ruled in the emperor's name
A religious leader who advised the emperor
A wandering warrior loyal to no lord

applies.prompt: "They cooked the vegetables over a small hibachi on the porch." What is a hibachi?

options.text: A portable charcoal grill or heater
